# Source Card: Long-term outcomes of patients with refractory cytopenia of childhood under observation only

## Summary
This peer-reviewed EWOG-MDS registry cohort (Blood Advances 2025; 100 pediatric patients with refractory cytopenia of childhood [RCC], normal karyotype, no germline predisposition, observed >=2 years from diagnosis) establishes that an observation-only approach is safe: 5- and 10-year transplant-free survival were 94% and 88%, and all patients were alive at median 7.2-year follow-up. For a pediatric CDS module it supplies (a) the EWOG-MDS scope-exit / early-HSCT-indication criteria (ANC <1×10^9/L, transfusion dependency, monosomy 7, del(7q), >=2 chromosomal aberrations) and (b) study-defined blood-count severity bands (significant vs mild) for Hb, ANC, and platelets. It also updates surveillance practice (blood counts every 3-6 months; yearly marrow can be omitted).

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)

- (ev_002) Early-HSCT (scope-exit) indication incl. ANC <1×10^9/L — locator: Introduction, risk-adapted approach — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:19d30250c979d8d80c252e878efc5295d6f358c9646d33c25adf73679ccc76de]"
- (ev_003) 'Significant' abnormality band Hb <10 g/dL, ANC <1×10^9/L, plt <100×10^9/L — locator: Results, Hematological course — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ad077bb99bc34ba4bc3a34544bbcc9e5f458a0d71eef8363c79a90d454b3d711]"
- (ev_004) 'Mild' abnormality band Hb 10-12 g/dL, ANC 1.0-1.5×10^9/L, plt 100-150×10^9/L — locator: Results, Hematological course — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c237be2e14b398794e38aa9879a94892bcdb58a8d227a3358ad42efd0f4061dc]"
- (ev_005) 5-/10-year transplant-free survival 94%/88%, all alive — locator: Results, Clinical outcome — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:6839be3f390ea6f0ce748462c2d87cedb2f0291e01f55bf7c4cd247e539e197d]"
- (ev_006) Surveillance: blood counts every 3-6 months; yearly marrow omittable — locator: Discussion — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:16fe9e235a4bd017df006c504695a5b50b1781dd4add493f969c34c77ab25ecd]"
- (ev_007) Later-HSCT patients had lower diagnosis-time Hb (8.7 vs 10.6 g/dL) and plt (30 vs 64 ×10^9/L) — locator: Results, Clinical outcome — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:88d7f6785091a9eafda211b87b1ff21535c8d28925597d9ed95806048c554d85]"
- (ev_008) Median age 10.9 y (1.4-17.3), M/F 1.2, MCV increased in ~half — locator: Results, Patient characteristics — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f8c438f4e4877f4fec4238e312062182954f96c80d3f03d1c8dbdbea57922606]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric RCC, age 1.4-17.3 y (median 10.9), both sexes; explicitly normal karyotype and germline-predisposition-excluded — the numeric bands do NOT generalize to RCC with monosomy 7/del(7q)/predisposition (those are stratified to HSCT).
- Assay: MCV is explicitly age-adjusted (not a fixed adult cutoff); ANC and platelet thresholds are analyzer-agnostic absolute counts. Diagnosis by central EWOG-MDS pathology review under WHO 2008/2017 and 2022 ICC.
- Lifecycle: severity bands and HSCT-indication criteria are from EWOG-MDS 98 / EWOG-MDS 2006 protocols (enrollment 1998-2021); the every-3-6-month blood-count surveillance recommendation (omitting yearly marrow) is a 2025 update superseding prior yearly-marrow practice.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- YES — ev_002 defines the observation-vs-HSCT boundary: transfusion dependency, ANC <1×10^9/L, monosomy 7, del(7q), or >=2 chromosomal aberrations are early-HSCT (scope-exit) triggers. ev_006 defines the monitoring trigger: deviation of 3-6-monthly blood counts from the patient's own baseline warrants further investigation (marrow study). These are source-stated triggers, not executable directives — a CDS converter must treat them as proposals for clinician review, not automated actions.

## Limitations & conflicts
- UNIT-RENDERING ARTIFACT (fidelity flag, resolved): PMC full-text extraction had dropped the superscript "9" on every published ×10^9/L figure. The affected count-unit quotes (ev_002, ev_003, ev_004, ev_007) were re-verified against the publisher JATS full text (Europe PMC PMC12395045) and corrected to the intact "× 10⁹/L"; UCUM units in each threshold block are the correct 10*9/L.
- The severity bands (ev_003/ev_004) are the study's own descriptive categorization of follow-up counts, not validated diagnostic cutoffs; ev_007 values are median group comparisons (n=9 vs 91), not decision thresholds. Do not convert either into a hard automated rule.
- Sex-specific and gestational/postnatal-age-specific reference values are NOT provided; missingness is explicit — this source does not supply neonatal or per-sex thresholds.
- Cohort is normal-karyotype, predisposition-excluded RCC only; conflicts with any generalized "RCC = observe" rule are preserved: abnormal-karyotype/predisposition RCC is stratified to HSCT and is out of scope here. No direct numeric conflict with other cards recorded; a comparator Japanese cohort (5-y OS 87% ± 9%, n=27) is cited by the authors as concordant.
